commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=11e18d136f35ed38f2976b7cbde3bb85c03f742c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `armTimer(state)` to both cron timer rejection handlers and adds a Vitest file for cron timer catch re-arm behavior.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +75. Total +81 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is established for the original multi-day macOS no-log stall. Source inspection shows the rejected timer-callback path on current main, but the PR proof does not demonstrate that path after the fix.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-owned runtime proof is still insufficient and the remaining code issue is review feedback, so this needs author and maintainer follow-up rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(cron): re-arm timer chain on unhandled rejection
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When onTimer rejects, the .catch handler logs the error but does not re-arm the timer. This permanently kills the cron scheduler until the next gateway restart. Inspired by #73355, rewritten against current main.

## Evidence

Fix deployed to running OpenClaw gateway (aa69b12). During this session the gateway was restarted 5+ times deploying patches. Each restart is the scenario that would previously kill the scheduler on any error.

Cron job list after 5+ restarts, all jobs still scheduled:
- Morning Brief: enabled, nextRunAt set, lastRunStatus null
- Repo Monitor: enabled, nextRunAt set, lastRunStatus error (ran, errored, re-armed)
- Daily R&D: enabled, nextRunAt set, lastRunStatus null

Key evidence: Repo Monitor has lastRunStatus error but nextRunAt is still set. The .catch handler called armTimer(state) which re-scheduled the next run. Without this fix, error would be the last event for the scheduler.

session_status: OpenClaw 2026.6.10, uptime 15m, session duration 3h35m, all plugins OK.

Fixes #73166. Test plan: pnpm test src/cron/service/timer.catch-rearm.test.ts
target.author: rizwan-saddal

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
